article_title: AI-Powered Sorting Facilities Are Reshaping the Recycling Industry as AMP's First Fully Automated Plant Nears Operation

content_preview.title: AI-Powered Sorting Facilities Are Reshaping the Recycling Industry as AMP's First Fully Automated Plant Nears Operation
content_preview.summary: AMP's Commerce City facility will process 62,000 tons of recycling annually with minimal human intervention, as AI sorting systems spread across the waste industry.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The recycling industry is undergoing a shift from labor-intensive manual sorting to AI-driven automation, and a facility nearing completion in Commerce City, Colorado, may represent the clearest example yet. Built by Waste Connections and operated by Louisville-based [AMP](https://ampsortation.com/articles/wcn-denver-groundbreaking), the plant is designed to process 62,000 tons of single-stream recycling per year with minimal human intervention, using the company's AMP ONE sortation...
